assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md structural summary (line 53): '-1 test function removed (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers)'. Reductive Signals section (line 62): 'Function removal: test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers was completely removed from tests/api/purl_recommend.rs. This function tested that qualifier-specific behavior returned separate entries for PURLs differing only in qualifiers and verified contains("repository_url=") on each entry. This test coverage is lost.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md structural summary (line 54): 'Assertions relaxed in test_recommend_purls_basic: the original assertion checked a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers ("pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"); the new assertion checks a versioned PURL without qualifiers ("pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"). While new contains("?") negative assertions were added, the specificity of the PURL value check was reduced.' Reductive Signals item 2 (line 64): 'Assertion relaxation: In test_recommend_purls_basic, the assertion on body.items[0].purl was changed from checking a fully qualified PURL (with ?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar) to checking only the versioned PURL (pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12). The assertion now checks a shorter, less specific value.' Semantic Assessment (line 73): 'assertions were both added and relaxed. The classification is therefore MIXED.'

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 47: 'Classification basis: File content comparison between base-branch and PR-branch versions of tests/api/purl_recommend.rs, plus new test file analysis.' The structural summary references specific function names (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers, test_recommend_purls_dedup), exact assertion values ('pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar' vs 'pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12'), and file-level changes (+1/-1 functions, new file with +3 functions). The reductive and additive signals sections reference concrete test file content (function bodies, assertion strings, contains checks). The semantic assessment discusses structural signals (test additions/removals, assertion relaxation) rather than acceptance criteria.
